summary_one_line: Input must carry the 4-byte FOURCC 'LuvK' (4C 75 76 4B) at offset 0x10 so the colorspace signature equals cmsSigLuvKData.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 4-byte colorspace-signature field at input offsets 0x10-0x13 equal to the FOURCC 'LuvK' (0x4C 0x75 0x76 0x4B), which makes cmsChannelsOfColorSpace switch on ColorSpace == cmsSigLuvKData and take the true side of the case at cmspcs.c:900.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed (value_profile_cmplog) holds 4c(L) at 0x10, 75(u) at 0x11, 76(v) at 0x12, 4b(K) at 0x13 — the ASCII 'LuvK' shown in the seed hex dumps — so the signature parsed from those bytes equals cmsSigLuvKData and the blocked true branch is taken.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds (value_profile) carry 'RGB ' (52(R) 47(G) 42(B) 20(space)) at the same offsets 0x10-0x13, which maps to cmsSigRgbData (return 3 at line 898), so the cmsSigLuvKData case is never reached and the default/3-channel arms are taken instead.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator over value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback: cmplog records the runtime comparison of the parsed 4-byte signature against the cmsSigLuvKData constant and splices that exact constant back into the matching input bytes at offset 0x10, jumping directly to 'LuvK'. value_profile's Hamming-distance CMP_MAP only rewards getting closer one bit at a time and cannot bridge a 4-byte exact-magic equality in this budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the switch case cmsSigLuvKData at cmspcs.c:900 whose true side is globally blocked.
Winner-resolving seeds contain ASCII 'LuvK' at offset 0x10.
All ten winner inputs hold the LuvK FOURCC bytes at offsets 0x10-0x13 while losers do not.
Loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' at the same offset 0x10 instead of LuvK.
The winner-only coverage region begins exactly at the cmsSigLuvKData case (4-channel arm); the loser instead reaches the 3-channel cases / default.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side many times while the loser takes only the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 900  T=21 F=19  T=0 F=58  case cmsSigLuvKData:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winning fuzzer value_profile_cmplog has the I2SRandReplace mutator, which value_profile lacks.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace; it relies only on havoc/token and CMP_MAP distance feedback.
value_profile's CMP_MAP only encodes how close operands are via a Hamming-distance bucket, not an exact substitution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close the two
operands were (Hamming distance bucket for `trace_cmp*`; matching-prefix
length for string/memory CMPs).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2299 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed e14684dce8ca51f3` and observed an I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 of the 4-level ancestor chain; the tool reported '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 3', characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 4-byte exact-magic equality (input == 'LuvK') in which value_profile resolves the branch at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog, showing CMP_MAP gradient feedback alone bridges the exact-magic gate without I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly confirm that the parser reads offsets 0x10-0x13 as the LuvK signature value rather than another field that merely correlates with it across these seeds.
